title: To W. R. Thick Is The Darkness
content:
Thick is the darkness -
    Sunward, O, sunward!
    Rough is the highway -
    Onward, still onward!

    Dawn harbours surely
    East of the shadows.
    Facing us somewhere
    Spread the sweet meadows.

    Upward and forward!
    Time will restore us:
    Light is above us,
    Rest is before us.

    1876
meaning:
"To W. R. Thick Is The Darkness" is a quintessential example of William Ernest Henley's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
